data.title: Regions Financial gains as lower provisions help Q1 earnings
data.description:
Regions Financial (RF) was trading higher as lower provisions boosted first-quarter earnings despite disappointing revenue.

Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $0.62 was above the consensus estimate by $0.03, while revenue of $1.87B (+4.9% Y/Y) missed by $50M.

Shares were 1.11% higher to $28.23 Friday pre-market.

Net interest income was up 4.5% year-over-year to $1.25B, but modestly trailed the consensus of $1.26B. Noninterest income of $625M compares with the average analyst estimate of $653.13M.

While total revenue has increased versus the first quarter of 2025, it decreased 2% on both a reported and adjusted basis compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, said the Q1 earnings release by RF.

Net interest income decreased 3%, driven primarily by fewer days in the quarter and elevated non-recurring items in the prior quarter that did not repeat. 

Net interest margin was also negatively impacted by tighter asset spreads associated with higher-quality asset growth, contributing to a 3 basis point decrease to 3.67%. The consensus stands at 3.69%.

Average loans hit $96.42B during the quarter, slightly above the S&P Cap IQ consensus of $96.03B. Average deposits stood at $130.23B, while the consensus for deposits is $131.54B.

Earnings call is scheduled for 10 AM ET today.

data.title: Krispy Kreme expands to the Netherlands as it grows its European presence
data.description:
Krispy Kreme (DNUT) announced an agreement with Jafa Holding BV to bring the brand to the Netherlands. The first Krispy Kreme store in the Netherlands is expected to open during the later part of this year. The expansion marks a significant milestone as the Company continues to grow globally through its capital-light franchise model.

The first Netherlands Hot Light Theater Shop will feature the brand’s iconic Hot Light experience and serve as both a retail location and a production hub. The chain expects approximately 30 locations to open over in the Netherlands over the next five years. In Europe, Krispy Kreme already has a presence in France, Ireland, Turkey, and the United Kingdom.

The expansion was noted to mark a significant milestone as the company continues to grow globally through its capital-light franchise model. Looking ahead, Krispy Kreme (DNUT) plans to open at least 100 shops globally and three to four new international markets this year, including the Netherlands, as it continues to work with experienced franchise operators.

Shares of Krispy Kreme (DNUT) are down 11.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Ally Financial Q1 earnings beat amid record consumer auto loan applications
data.description:
Ally Financial (ALLY) stock climbed 5.3% in Friday premarket trading after the company posted Q1 earnings exceeding Wall Street expectations and reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

The bank and auto lender still expects 2026 net interest margin (excluding original issue discount) of 3.60%-3.70%, net charge-offs for retail auto of 1.8%-2.0%, and a consolidated net charge-off rate of 1.2%-1.4%.

In dealer financial services, strong dealer relationships led to a record 4.4M consumer applications. The robust application flow allowed the company to remain selective while delivering originations of $11.5B, a 13% Y/Y increase despite lower industry sales, the company said.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.11, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.94, rose from $1.09 in Q4 2025 and $0.58 in last year’s Q1.

Q1 adjusted total net revenue of $2.18B, beating the $2.14B consensus, increased from $2.17B in the prior quarter and $2.07B in the year-ago period.

Adjusted provision for credit losses of $474M dropped from $487M in Q4 and $497M in Q1 2025.

Adjusted noninterest expense of $1.24B rose from $1.22B in the previous quarter and fell from $1.32B a year ago.

Net financing revenue (excluding core OID) was $1.61B, down from $1.62B in Q4 and up from $1.49B in last year’s Q1.

Core return on tangible common equity was 11.1%, unchanged from the previous quarter and up from 6.7% a year ago.

Consumer auto loan originations of $11.5B included $7.5B of used retail volume, or 66% of total originations, $3.2B of new retail volume, and $720M of lease. Estimated retail auto originated yield was 9.60% in Q1, with 41% of originations in Ally’s (ALLY) highest credit quality tier.

Pretax income by segment:

  • Auto Finance: $336M vs. $372M in Q4 and $375M in last year’s Q1.

  • Insurance: $28M vs. $91M in the prior quarter and $2M a year ago.

  • Corporate Finance: $94M vs. $98M in Q4 and $76M in Q1 2025.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

data.description: • Reconnaissance Energy Africa Ltd. press release (RECO:CA): FY GAAP EPS of -C$0.05.

data.title: APi Group buys Wtech Fire Group
data.description:
• APi Group (APG) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Wtech Fire Group, an Ireland-based provider of fire protection, suppression, and detection solutions across Europe.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions, including receipt of required regulatory approvals.
  • Wtech is expected to contribute approximately $175M in annual revenue, with a margin profile consistent with APi’s international business.
  • Russ Becker, APi’s president and chief executive officer, stated: “We are excited to welcome the Wtech team to the APi family. Our international business has built real strength in fire alarm and detection, and electronic security, but fire sprinkler and suppression is an area where we need greater scale to truly serve our customers end-to-end. Wtech fills that gap nicely, and their expertise in this space makes them a natural fit.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Immuron Q3 sales rise 16% to A$1.5M
data.description:
• Immuron reported third-quarter global sales of A$1.5 million, up 16% from the prior comparative period, while year-to-date sales through March 2026 rose 7% to A$5.7 million.
  • In the US, Q3 sales came in at A$0.5 million, up 1% from the prior comparative period, while year-to-date sales through March 2026 rose 10% to A$1.3 million.

data.title: State Street Non-GAAP EPS of $2.84 beats by $0.20, revenue of $3.8B beats by $110M
data.description:
• State Street press release (STT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.84 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $3.8B (+15.9% Y/Y) beats by $110M.
  • Investment Servicing AUC/A as of quarter-end increased 17% to $54.5 trillion, mainly due to higher market levels, flows, and net new business
     
  • Investment Management AUM as of quarter-end increased 20% to $5.6 trillion, mainly driven by higher market levels and net inflows

data.title: Lilly weight loss pill prescriptions said to near 1,400 in first week of launch
data.description:
• U.S. prescriptions for Eli Lilly’s (LLY) new weight loss pill, Foundayo, reached 1,390 for the week ending April 10, after the Indiana-based drugmaker launched the GLP-1 product earlier this month, Reuters reported on Friday, citing IQVIA (IQV) data sourced from an analyst.
  • The prescriptions for Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) rival therapy, the Wegovy pill, approached 113,354 over the same week in the U.S. compared to 105,366 prescriptions in the previous week, according to data from the healthcare analytics company.
  • The Danish drugmaker’s product was prescribed 3,071 times in the U.S. following its market debut on January 5.
  • The U.S. FDA approved Foundayo as a once-daily therapy for obese or overweight adults with weight-related medical problems on April 1, and the company went on to immediately accept prescriptions through its direct-to-consumer site, LillyDirect.

data.title: Tesla plans to sell a larger Model Y variant in India
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is making plans to unveil a new, larger version of the Model Y in India as early as next week, according to Bloomberg. 

The Model Y L will be Tesla's (TSLA) first new product in India since its market entry. The Tesla Model Y L is the long-wheelbase, six-seat version of the Model Y that Tesla (TSLA) first launched in China, and it adds a roomier third row plus more family-focused practicality. 

The company plans to ship the new Model Y variant to India from the Gigafactory Shanghai, which is the only site making the SUV so far.

The expanded lineup in India by Tesla (TSLA) is reportedly part of a broader effort to refresh its portfolio without launching entirely new platforms. Sales in India for Tesla (TSLA) are very light, due in part to high tariffs.  

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) edge 0.5% higher in premarket trading on Friday to $391.22 vs. the 52-week range of $222.79 to $498.33. The company is due to report first-quarter earnings next week.
